title: Transient resource cleanup in EccKey::create error paths is correct
description: key.rs::create() uses map_err to flush the SRK transient slot before propagating any error from the key template setup or CreateAndLoadKey steps. This prevents transient object slot leaks under error conditions even before the EccKey struct exists (so its Drop cannot run). The pattern of 'flush on error before the owning struct is constructed' is correct here and should not be replaced with RAII unless a temporary wrapper is introduced.
